title: alnus incana subsp. tenuifolia
intro: Alnus incana subsp. tenuifolia, also known as narrow-leaved gray alder, is a subspecies of the deciduous tree Alnus incana that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The narrow-leaved gray alder has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Alnus incana subsp. tenuifolia, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, are traditionally used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
